summary: SCH4U acid–base problems find pH and pOH. A strong acid ionizes completely, so [H⁺] equals its concentration; a weak acid needs its Ka and the approximation [H⁺] ≈ √(Ka·c).
method: Decide whether the acid is strong (ionizes fully) or weak (partial, needs Ka).
Strong acid: set [H⁺] equal to the acid concentration.
Weak acid: approximate [H⁺] ≈ √(Ka·c) from the equilibrium HA ⇌ H⁺ + A⁻.
If asked for pOH, use pH + pOH = 14 at 25 °C.

example.title: pH of a weak acetic-acid solution
steps: Acetic acid is weak, so use [H⁺] ≈ √(Ka·c).
